feed.title: [BIP Draft] 24 bits for nVersion nonce space instead of 16

entry.title: [BIP Draft] 24 bits for nVersion nonce space instead of 16
entry.summary:
In a recent discussion on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List, Antoine Poinsot addressed a technical aspect of Bitcoin mining that concerns the efficiency and capability of ASIC miners. The conversation revolved around the current limitations imposed by the 16 bits in the header, which currently allow ASICs to receive a new job only once every second, accommodating up to approximately 280TH/s. This limitation is becoming increasingly significant as the performance of ASICs has surpassed this threshold, with some models now capable of achieving over 1PH/s.
Poinsot proposed an enhancement to this system by suggesting the addition of 8 more bits to the header. This adjustment would significantly expand the capacity, enabling the system to support ASIC mining operations up to 72PH/s without the need for nTime rolling. Furthermore, this change would also provide 5 bits for concurrent deployments, thereby offering flexibility for multiple operations to run simultaneously.
The suggestion implies a forward-looking approach to Bitcoin's development, considering the pace at which mining technology is advancing. With Bitcoin's evolution and the maturation of its underlying technology, upgrades are becoming less frequent, indicating a stabilization phase. In this context, Poinsot's proposal aims to future-proof the network against the backdrop of rapidly evolving ASIC capabilities, ensuring that the infrastructure can accommodate continued growth in mining power without necessitating constant adjustments. This initiative reflects a proactive stance in addressing scalability and efficiency challenges within the Bitcoin mining ecosystem.
